The Inflection

Every commodity market followed this path.

Asset Standard Enabled Scale
WTI (1983) NYMEX futures Hedging + price discovery $10T+/yr
VIX (1993) CBOE options Vol trading + risk mgmt $2B+/day
BTC (2016) CF Benchmarks BRR CME licensed → Kraken ~$100M $100M exit

GPU compute is next. $723B in annual OpEx on cloud services (Gartner, 2025). No settlement standard. No derivatives. No hedging.

No pricing standard.

Market Structure

The right side of the market map is completely empty.

Layer What Exists Who Status
Price Data Per-GPU spot/OD feeds Silicon Data, Vast, etc. Infra Ready
Per-GPU Derivatives GPU-specific futures Ornn, Architect Infra Ready
Cross-GPU Standard NOTHING — YFT
OTC Swaps NOTHING — Positioned
Clearing NOTHING — Positioned
Exchange Listing NOTHING — Positioned

Yggdrasil is the only cross-GPU pricing standard.

The Problem

No standard. No visibility. No hedge.
Three unsolved problems in the world's fastest-growing commodity market.

No Pricing Standard

  • 250+ providers, ~60 chip architectures — no shared benchmark
  • H100 ≠ MI300X at identical $/hr — apples vs. oranges
  • Training and inference have different cost physics — no index captures both

Degradation Is Invisible

  • GPU performance degrades over chip lifetime — no market standard exists to track it
  • Buyers pay spec-sheet prices for hardware running below spec
  • No market publishes performance-adjusted pricing

Exposure Can't Be Hedged

  • $100B+/yr in compute spend locked into EDPs, MACCs, CUDs
  • Prices move — no futures, no OTC swap, no ETF to offset risk
  • Without a standard index, no instrument can exist
The Solution

The Compute Unit Index.
The WTI for GPU compute.

Compute Unit Index

  • CU — Composite
  • TCU — Training
  • ICU — Inference
  • Bound by physics-based constraints

Degradation Benchmarks

Know exactly how much compute each instance is yielding

OTC Swaps

Financial indices to solve unhedgeable exposure

WTI
→
CU

WTI grades every barrel of oil.
CU grades every compute-hour.

IP Protection

The methodology is patent pending.
The moat is real.

  • Non-provisional filed — USPTO review active · Track One (~9 months)
  • Core algorithm protected — geometric mean normalization, physics-calibrated 4-parameter scoring
  • Empirically calibrated — TCU / ICU scoring validated against real compute workloads, not manufacturer spec sheets. Others collect specs. We measure reality.
  • Can't replicate without licensing — values published, method protected

not a benchmark — needs an index to settle
Yggdrasil
$0 raised · Seeking $3M
Patent-pending normalization + live product + Goldman product direction
No competitor has cross-architecture normalization — the prerequisite for exchange-grade settlement.

How It Works

A financial overlay on physical compute contracts.

Physical Layer — Unchanged
AWS EDP
Enterprise Discount Program
Azure MACC
Consumption Commitment
GCP CUD
Committed Use Discounts
+ Independent Financial Overlay
CU Swap — Contract for Differences

Enterprise pays: Fixed CU rate

Counterparty pays: Floating CU Index, monthly

Settlement: Net cash difference — no GPU changes hands

Exit: Sell the position. No penalties. No ratchets.

Buys Fixed
Cloud Providers · Hardware Owners
Revenue tied to spot prices.
Swap pays them when spot drops.
Bank Earns Spread
Sells Fixed
AI Labs · Enterprises
Costs exposed to spot spikes.
Swap pays them when spot rises.

Same structure as energy VPPAs.
Google, Microsoft, and Amazon already use these for electricity.

Vision

Every compute transaction in the world prices against this index.

I Now → Series A
OTC Market
30–40 bps · novel market

Bilateral ISDA swaps. Bloomberg tickers live. Prove demand — build the history CME needs.

II Series A → B
CME Listing
$0.05–$0.10 / contract

CU / TCU / ICU futures on CME. CME Clearing handles DCO. CF Benchmarks → BRR precedent.

III Series C
Yggdrasil Clearing
Index + clearing fees

CFTC-registered DCO. Full stack — index, exchange standard, clearing rail. VIX model: $100M+/yr.

IV Long-term
Asia Expansion
Second standard

~$1T unhedged compute behind the firewall. No standardized pricing exists. A generational second market.

Data Subscriptions — Running the entire way through every act. Cash flow from Day 1, compounding with brand recognition at every stage. $45–90K MRR → $150K+ → scales with exchange volume

CF Benchmarks built the Bitcoin benchmark. CME licensed it. Kraken paid $100M to own it.
We're building the compute equivalent — for a market 10× larger.
